hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery and reporting become more deterministic if every retained `recorded_at` timestamp is locked to exact UTC ISO-8601 form, so lexicographic recency checks cannot drift on malformed or locale-shaped strings.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` used lexicographic `recorded_at` comparison to choose the latest kept or rejected learning entry, but it accepted arbitrary string timestamps across terminal state, learning_log, and hypothesis-card surfaces, so a synchronized falsification that rewrote `sysopt.command-ampersand-shell-safety-lock` timestamps to a non-canonical value such as `2026-4-6T3:36:42Z` could still verify clean while weakening deterministic recency selection. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any non-UTC ISO `recorded_at` in retained systems-optimizer state, learning_log, or hypothesis-card artifacts while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if non-UTC or non-canonical `recorded_at` strings fail verification across retained systems-optimizer artifacts and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer `recorded_at` fields must use exact UTC ISO-8601 timestamps; recency locks are only deterministic when every retained artifact shares one canonical sortable clock format.
